title: Systems and methods for disabling page scans
abstract: A method for wireless communication by a Bluetooth device is described. The method includes determining a paired device configuration for Bluetooth communication. The method also includes disabling Bluetooth page scans based on the paired device configuration. The page scans may be disabled when the Bluetooth device is in a connectable state for Bluetooth communication.
claims: Claims 1. A method for wireless communication by a Bluetooth device, comprising: determining a paired device configuration for Bluetooth communication; and disabling Bluetooth page scans based on the paired device configuration when the Bluetooth device is in a connectable state for Bluetooth communication. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when Bluetooth communication is on and the Bluetooth device has no paired devices. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when a paired device list does not contain any paired device profiles. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when all paired device profiles have been removed from the Bluetooth device. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when the paired device configuration comprises Bluetooth communication is on and no paired device has attempted to connect to the Bluetooth device within a threshold time period. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the paired device configuration comprises determining a paired device connection history, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled at a specific time of day based on the paired device connection history for that time of day. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when the paired device configuration comprises all paired devices are connected to the Bluetooth device. 8. The method of claim 7, further comprising applying resources made available by disabling the page scans to improve the quality of service of existing connections to the paired devices. 9. A Bluetooth device, comprising: a processor; memory in electronic communication with the processor; and instructions stored in the memory, the instructions being executable by the processor to: determine a paired device configuration for Bluetooth communication; and disable Bluetooth page scans based on the paired device configuration when the Bluetooth device is in a connectable state for Bluetooth communication. 10. The Bluetooth device of claim 9, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when Bluetooth communication is on and the Bluetooth device has no paired devices. 11. The Bluetooth device of claim 10, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when a paired device list does not contain any paired device profiles. 12. The Bluetooth device of claim 10, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when all paired device profiles have been removed from the Bluetooth device. 13. The Bluetooth device of claim 9, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when the paired device configuration comprises Bluetooth communication is on and no paired device has attempted to connect to the Bluetooth device within a threshold time period. 14. The Bluetooth device of claim 9, wherein the instructions executable to determine the paired device configuration comprise instructions executable to determine a paired device connection history, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled at a specific time of day based on the paired device connection history for that time of day. 15. The Bluetooth device of claim 9, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when the paired device configuration comprises all paired devices are connected to the Bluetooth device. 16. The Bluetooth device of claim 15, further comprising instructions executable to apply resources made available by disabling the page scans to improve the quality of service of existing connections to the paired devices. 17. An apparatus for wireless communication, comprising: means for determining a paired device configuration for Bluetooth communication; and means for disabling Bluetooth page scans based on the paired device configuration when the apparatus is in a connectable state for Bluetooth communication. 18. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when Bluetooth communication is on and the apparatus has no paired devices. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when a paired device list does not contain any paired device profiles. 20. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when all paired device profiles have been removed from the apparatus. 21. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when the paired device configuration comprises Bluetooth communication is on and no paired device has attempted to connect to the apparatus within a threshold time period. 22. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the means for determining the paired device configuration comprise means for determining a paired device connection history, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled at a specific time of day based on the paired device connection history for that time of day. 23. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when the paired device configuration comprises all paired devices are connected to the apparatus. 24. A computer-program product for wireless communication, the computer- program product comprising a non-transitory computer-readable medium having instructions thereon, the instructions comprising: code for causing a Bluetooth device to determine a paired device configuration for Bluetooth communication; and code for causing the Bluetooth device to disable Bluetooth page scans based on the paired device configuration when the Bluetooth device is in a connectable state for Bluetooth communication. 25. The computer-program product of claim 24, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when Bluetooth communication is on and the Bluetooth device has no paired devices. 26. The computer-program product of claim 25, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when a paired device list does not contain any paired device profiles. 27. The computer-program product of claim 25, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when all paired device profiles have been removed from the Bluetooth device. 28. The computer-program product of claim 24, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when the paired device configuration comprises Bluetooth communication is on and no paired device has attempted to connect to the Bluetooth device within a threshold time period. 29. The computer-program product of claim 24, wherein the code for causing the Bluetooth device to determine the paired device configuration comprises code for causing the Bluetooth device to determine a paired device connection history, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled at a specific time of day based on the paired device connection history for that time of day. 30. The computer-program product of claim 24, wherein Bluetooth page scans are disabled when the paired device configuration comprises all paired devices are connected to the Bluetooth device.
